Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

The original fix for this issue (3917e620) introduced test conf-sanity #77 which proved to be wrong, but was passing nicely. You need to also fix conf-sanity to have a good test which really check this case.
test #77 just checks the mdt and osts start, but do not check they start with the good configuration (which was the defect for the original commit).

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.5/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/f7fc9a36-92db-11e3-8842-52540035b04c).  Ran 21 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity.

Patch Set 5: Code-Review-1

You may have introduced a regression compared to patchset 2 (maybe when dealing with s1 and s3), as my regression tests show that patchset 5 does not fix the issue (while patchset 2 did).

Patch Set 5:

Replacing "s3" with "s1" in patch set 5 introduced the following regression failure:

kernel: LDISKFS-fs (dm-0): Unrecognized mount option "param=sys.timeout=20param=lov.stripesize=1048576param=lov.stripecount=0param=mdt.identity_upcall=/usr/sbin/l_getidentity" or missing value

Debug log:

(obd_mount.c:853:lmd_print()) options: user_xattr,acl,errors=remount-ro,iopen_nopriv,user_xattr,acl,param=sys.timeout=20param=lov.stripesize=1048576param=lov.stripecount=0param=mdt.identity_upcall=/usr/sbin/l_getidentity

I'll investigate the cause.

Patch Set 5:

I just found the root cause.

In the above failure, the original options were:

user_xattr,acl,osd=osd-ldiskfs,errors=remount-ro,iopen_nopriv,user_xattr,acl,mgs,virgin,update,param=sys.timeout=20,param=lov.stripesize=1048576,param=lov.stripecount=0,param=mdt.identity_upcall=/usr/sbin/l_getidentity,svname=lustre-MDT0000,device=/dev/lvm-Role_MDS/P1

After parsing "param=sys.timeout=20", "s1" pointed to ",param=lov.stripesize=1048576,param=lov.stripecount=0,...", and "s2" pointed to "param=lov.stripesize=1048576,param=lov.stripecount=0,...".

Then memmove(s1, s2, strlen(s2) + 1) made the options become "...param=sys.timeout=20param=lov.stripesize=1048576...".

In fact, "s1" should point to "param=sys.timeout=20,param=lov.stripesize=1048576,param=lov.stripecount=0,...", which will make the memmove operation clear the "param=sys.timeout=20" option correctly.

So, it turns out that the value of "s1" in the long "if ... else if ..." code block should not be changed, and "s3" is still needed. I'll revise the patch back to the changes in patch set 4.
Uploaded patch set 6.

Patch Set 6:

This patch set is the same as patch set 4.

parents.subject: LU-4471 mdd: mdd_unlink: do trans_start after sanity check

commit.subject: LU-4460 mount: fix lmd_parse() to handle comma-separated NIDs
commit.message:
LU-4460 mount: fix lmd_parse() to handle comma-separated NIDs

This patch reverts commit 3917e62018878dfffac59ceed70f20b0419945d3,
which cannot handle the upgrade situation that old mountdata already
contains comma-separated NIDs. The correct way to fix the original
issue is to parse comma-separated NIDs in lmd_parse().

The patch also updates disk2_4-ldiskfs.tar.bz2 to make the mountdata
of ost contain comma-separated NIDs so as to verify the patch under
upgrade situation.

Test-Parameters: alwaysuploadlogs \
envdefinitions=SLOW=yes,ENABLE_QUOTA=yes testlist=conf-sanity

Signed-off-by: Jian Yu <jian.yu@intel.com>
Change-Id: If179618c9c89dc2168f748aeba59384ea31197ff
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/8918
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Niu Yawei <yawei.niu@intel.com>

Reviewed-by: Sebastien Buisson <sebastien.buisson@bull.net>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
